mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 500,000 signals rapid labor market recovery, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of months where payroll growth exceeds the 500k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, which sustains corporate revenue growth and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of observed quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side contraction that immediately triggers risk-off positioning and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of instances when industrial production falls below its annual trend.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates increase economic uncertainty and reduce household consumption capacity, leading to lower corporate earnings forecasts and negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 60 percent of months following unemployment rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0 percent year-over-year, markets price in imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where inflation exceeds the 3 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where real GDP growth accelerates while housing starts decelerate indicates a shift from residential to broader economic activity, supporting stable corporate earnings and positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: The GDP-housing divergence signal yields negative returns in more than 60 percent of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and business investment, which directly translates to higher quarterly corporate profits and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of periods following positive industrial production growth.
